description: Canonical terms and definitions used across the Axelar cross-chain interoperability platform and its APIs

terms.label: General Message Passing
terms.definition: Axelar's protocol that allows smart contracts on one chain to call and pass arbitrary data to contracts on any other supported chain, enabling complex cross-chain dApp logic.

terms.label: Interchain Token Service
terms.definition: Axelar's system for deploying tokens that are natively interoperable across multiple chains, preserving canonical supply and supporting custom token managers.

terms.label: Axelar Token
terms.definition: The native utility and governance token of the Axelar network, used for gas payments, validator staking, and on-chain governance proposals.

terms.label: Axelar Gateway Contract
terms.definition: A smart contract deployed on each connected blockchain that serves as the entry and exit point for cross-chain messages and token transfers routed through Axelar.

terms.label: Axelar Amplifier
terms.definition: The programmable integration layer that allows external chains and off-chain verifier networks to connect to Axelar without requiring core protocol changes, using a set of CosmWasm smart contracts.

terms.label: Amplifier GMP API
terms.definition: The HTTP REST API exposed by the Amplifier relayer service that external chain integrators and relayers use to publish on-chain events and poll pending transaction tasks.

terms.label: Cross-Chain Relayer
terms.definition: An off-chain service responsible for monitoring source chains, calling the Amplifier GMP API to submit events, polling for pending tasks, and submitting transactions to destination chains.

terms.label: Task Item
terms.definition: A pending on-chain transaction (such as EXECUTE, APPROVE_MESSAGES, or ROTATE_SIGNERS) returned by the Amplifier relayer that a relayer must submit to the destination chain.

terms.label: Broadcast ID
terms.definition: A UUID that uniquely identifies a CosmWasm MsgExecuteContract submission to the Axelar chain through the Amplifier relayer. Used to poll broadcast status.

terms.label: Cross-Chain Payload
terms.definition: Arbitrary byte data attached to a GMP call that is passed from the source contract to the destination contract on the receiving chain. Large payloads can be pre-stored by hash via the /payloads endpoint.

terms.label: Payload Hash
terms.definition: The keccak256 hash of a cross-chain message payload. Committed on the source chain and used by the Axelar network for verification before the payload is delivered on the destination chain.

terms.label: Signers Rotated Event
terms.definition: An on-chain event indicating that the multisig threshold signing set for a connected chain's Axelar prover has been updated to a new epoch.

terms.label: Axelarscan Explorer & API
terms.definition: The block explorer and analytics API for the Axelar network, providing real-time data on validators, GMP transactions, token transfers, TVL, and network health.

terms.label: Total Value Locked
terms.definition: The aggregate value of assets held by Axelar Gateway contracts across all connected chains, used as a measure of the network's economic security and adoption.

terms.label: Axelar Validator
terms.definition: A node operator that participates in Axelar's proof-of-stake consensus, votes on cross-chain events, signs cross-chain transactions, and earns AXL rewards. Metrics are exposed via the Validator API.

terms.label: Event ID
terms.definition: A unique string identifier assigned to each on-chain event submitted to the Amplifier GMP API, used to deduplicate and track event processing state.

terms.label: Deposit Address
terms.definition: A one-time Axelar-generated address on a source chain to which a user sends tokens for cross-chain transfer. Generated by AxelarAssetTransfer in the AxelarJS SDK.

terms.label: CosmWasm
terms.definition: A smart contract platform built on the Cosmos SDK. Axelar uses CosmWasm contracts internally for the Amplifier integration layer, including the Router, Gateway, and Prover contracts.
